title: Executive Order 8773—Authorizing and Directing the Secretary of War to Take Possession of and Operate the Inglewood Plant of North American Aviation, Inc.

flags.justification: The Secretary of War is authorized to take possession, operate the plant 'through such person or persons as he may designate,' and 'do all things necessary or incidental thereto' — broad, largely unchecked operational authority.

Termination occurs when the President determines the plant 'will be privately operated in a manner consistent with the needs of the national defense' — an entirely self-assessed standard with no independent review and no outer time bound.
Single coherent purpose: seizure of this one plant to resolve this one labor dispute and resume production.
'To do all things necessary or incidental thereto' is an open-ended operational standard with no defined limits.

No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable. Sub-flag does not apply — the seizure is a discrete, strike-specific intervention terminating on its own resolution, not an authority whose continuation depends on perpetuating the cited national emergency.

Termination is governed entirely by presidential self-assessment with no outer time bound — a genuine, unaddressed review gap for what is explicitly a temporary labor-dispute intervention, not an evergreen policy.
The strike itself, not this order, disrupted the ongoing National Defense Mediation Board process; the order responds to an already-stalled mediation, it does not bypass it.
No external party has a specified role in checking whether or when the plant should be returned to private operation — purely presidential self-assessment.

One of the first major WWII-era federal seizures of a private plant to resolve a labor dispute framed as a national-security necessity — sets a significant precedent for this mode of federal intervention that recurs repeatedly later in the corpus (see EO 9540, EO 9570).
The Secretary of War exercises seizure authority directly; no other agency's operational mandate is redirected.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 6/20 = 30.0%. A well-known historical seizure (the Inglewood/North American Aviation strike-breaking order); structurally similar to the later labor-dispute seizure orders in this corpus (EO 9540, EO 9570).
